intent: Fix the CI failure introduced by a12e696: bring files back under the repo's 500-line limit and make the entry-point test order-independent, with no behaviour change.
scope: python_pkg/brother_printer/consumables.py: new module holding consumable life tracking
Non-goal: any behaviour change; a12e696's verified reporting is untouched
Non-goal: focus_mode_daemon.py (509 lines, breached in 22e22c1, different package)
changes: Extract page counters, replacement baselines, the scale migration, and dropped-page detection from cups_service.py into consumables.py. The seam is real: none of it depends on CUPS service control, and cups_service only needs estimate_consumable_life for its fallback query, so the dependency runs one way with no cycle. cups_service.py 659 -> 414 lines, consumables.py 281.

Split the test files along the same seams: test_consumables.py (state/estimates/resets) and test_consumables_part2.py (counter scales and dropped pages); move page-count and dropped-page display tests to test_display_part3.py; move the PJL wire-protocol tests to test_usb_query_part2.py.

test_main_entry: pop python_pkg.brother_printer.__main__ from sys.modules before runpy.run_module. runpy emits a RuntimeWarning when the module is already imported, pytest is configured with filterwarnings=error, and the suite runs in random order - so the test passed or failed depending on whether the sibling import test ran first.

verification.command: python3 -m pytest python_pkg/ (full suite, as CI runs it)
verification.evidence: 692 passed; Required test coverage of 100.0% reached. Total coverage: 100.00%. The only local failure is test_file_length hitting a UnicodeDecodeError on an untracked .ci-mirror-venv/ file absent from CI's checkout.

verification.command: pytest test_main_entry ordered import-first (the order CI hit)
verification.evidence: Previously reproduced the CI failure exactly: RuntimeWarning: '...__main__' found in sys.modules after import of package. Now passes in either order.
No file owned by this change exceeds 500 lines. Only linux_configuration/.../focus_mode_daemon.py (509) remains, from commit 22e22c1.
Unchanged after the split: '. Sleep (standby)' CODE=40000, total 2087 pages, toner 68%, drum 80%.
All hooks pass (ruff, ruff format, mypy, pylint, bandit, coverage enforcement).

risks: Import churn: display.py and check_brother_printer.py now import consumables directly. Verified no cycle, and the full suite plus a real run exercise both paths.
The split is structural only; no logic moved between functions. Coverage remained at 100% throughout, so no branch lost its test.

CI will stay red until focus_mode_daemon.py (509 lines) is addressed. That breach predates this change and belongs to another package; it lay latent because the Python-tests workflow only triggers on python_pkg/** changes.
rollback: git revert <commit> restores the single-module layout; a12e696's behaviour is unaffected either way.
After rollback, validate with ./run.sh and expect the 500-line test to fail again on cups_service.py.
